page_content:
HONR300 Intergroup Dialogue Facilitator Development & Practicum (3 Credits) Facilitation is an artform and a science. This course is designed to introduce students to foundational dialogue facilitation skills and social justice frameworks needed to effectively facilitate group interactions across and within social differences. Students will apply the skills learned by facilitating a multi-session dialogue course with either the Common Ground or the Words of Engagement Intergroup Dialogue Program (WeIDP). Cross-listed with: EDDI300, CPSP300. Restriction: Permission of the Counseling, Higher Education, and Special Education department. Credit Only Granted for: WEID300, CPSP300, HONR300, or EDDI300. Formerly: WEID300. Additional Information: Students must be given permission to enroll and be admitted to the intergroup dialogue facilitator program.

HONR318 Advanced Honors Seminar (3 Credits) A series of seminars, often interdisciplinary in character, and sometimes team taught. The subjects will vary from semester to semester. Restriction: Permission of University Honors Program. Repeatable to: 9 credits if content differs.

HONR359 Honors Workshop (1-6 Credits) Honors workshops are small seminar classes which concentrate on skill development. Restriction: Permission of University Honors Program. Repeatable to: 9 credits if content differs.
